diff --git a/CLAUDE.md b/CLAUDE.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..1ec5eb7
--- /dev/null
+++ b/CLAUDE.md
@@ -0,0 +1,34 @@
+# Project Guidelines
+
+## Architecture
+- This project follows the repository pattern for data access
+- Services depend on repositories, never on each other directly
+- Events are preferred over direct cross-service calls for decoupling
+- All business logic lives in the service layer
+
+## Coding Conventions
+- Use TypeScript strict mode — no `any` types
+- All public methods must have JSDoc comments
+- Use dependency injection via constructor parameters
+- Error handling: throw typed errors, never return null for errors
+- Prefer async/await over raw Promises
+
+## Testing
+- Tests use vitest
+- Each module has a corresponding test file in tests/
+- Mock external dependencies, test business logic directly
+- Minimum: test the happy path and one error path per public method
+
+## File Organization
+- src/models/ — Data models and interfaces
+- src/repositories/ — Data access layer (implements repository interfaces)
+- src/services/ — Business logic layer
+- src/events/ — Event definitions and event bus
+- src/utils/ — Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
+- src/config/ — Configuration files
+- tests/ — Test files mirroring src/ structure
+
+## Git Practices
+- Commit atomically per logical change
+- Write descriptive commit messages explaining the "why"
+- Run tests before committing

diff --git a/src/models/notification.ts b/src/models/notification.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..290c865
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/models/notification.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,90 @@
+/**
+ * Notification model — core types for the notification system.
+ *
+ * Architecture: All notifications flow through the EventBus. Services emit
+ * domain events (e.g., order:created), and the NotificationService subscribes
+ * to those events and dispatches notifications through registered adapters.
+ *
+ * Constraints:
+ * - All delivery adapters must implement the NotificationAdapter interface.
+ * - All notifications must go through EventBus.emit() — no direct service calls.
+ * - Each NotificationChannel has exactly one adapter registered for it.
+ */
+
+/**
+ * Supported notification delivery channels.
+ * New channels (e.g., 'sms', 'push', 'slack') can be added here
+ * and a corresponding adapter registered with the NotificationService.
+ */
+export type NotificationChannel = 'email' | 'in-app';
+
+/**
+ * Notification priority levels.
+ */
+export type NotificationPriority = 'low' | 'normal' | 'high' | 'urgent';
+
+/**
+ * The type of event that triggered the notification.
+ */
+export type NotificationType =
+  | 'order-confirmation'
+  | 'order-status-update'
+  | 'welcome'
+  | 'account-updated';
+
+/**
+ * Payload passed to notification adapters for delivery.
+ */
+export interface NotificationPayload {
+  /** Unique notification ID. */
+  id: string;
+  /** What triggered this notification. */
+  type: NotificationType;
+  /** Delivery channel for this notification. */
+  channel: NotificationChannel;
+  /** Recipient identifier (email address, user ID, etc. depending on channel). */
+  recipient: string;
+  /** Notification subject line. */
+  subject: string;
+  /** Notification body content. */
+  body: string;
+  /** Priority level. */
+  priority: NotificationPriority;
+  /** When the notification was created. */
+  createdAt: Date;
+  /** Arbitrary metadata from the originating event. */
+  metadata?: Record<string, unknown>;
+}
+
+/**
+ * Result returned by an adapter after attempting delivery.
+ */
+export interface NotificationResult {
+  /** Whether delivery succeeded. */
+  success: boolean;
+  /** The notification that was sent. */
+  notification: NotificationPayload;
+  /** When delivery was attempted. */
+  deliveredAt: Date;
+  /** Error message if delivery failed. */
+  error?: string;
+}
+
+/**
+ * Interface that all notification delivery adapters must implement.
+ *
+ * To add a new notification channel:
+ * 1. Add the channel name to the NotificationChannel type above.
+ * 2. Create an adapter class implementing this interface.
+ * 3. Register it with NotificationService.registerAdapter().
+ */
+export interface NotificationAdapter {
+  /** Which channel this adapter handles. */
+  readonly channel: NotificationChannel;
+
+  /**
+   * Send a notification through this channel.
+   * Must return a NotificationResult indicating success or failure.
+   */
+  send(payload: NotificationPayload): Promise<NotificationResult>;
+}

prompt:
You are Agent 1 of 12 working on a codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-Uw0U9Z.

## Codebase Orientation
This is a TypeScript service (~1,140 lines, ~24 files).

Directory structure:
```
src/
  models/        # Data models and interfaces
  services/      # Business logic layer
  repositories/  # Data access layer
  utils/         # Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
  events/        # Event definitions (if using event-driven pattern)
  adapters/      # External service adapters (email, webhook, etc.)
  validators/    # Validation logic
  config/        # Configuration
tests/           # Test files mirroring src/ structure
```

Your task: Design and implement the foundation of a notification system.

Specifically:
1. Choose an architecture for notifications: event-driven (EventBus with subscribers) OR direct service calls. Commit to ONE approach.
2. Define the notification service interface and core types (NotificationType, NotificationPayload, etc.) in src/services/ and src/models/.
3. Implement the base notification service with the chosen architecture.
4. Create an email notification adapter in src/adapters/email.adapter.ts (stub is fine, but interface must be real).
5. Document your architectural decision — the pattern you chose, why, and what constraints it imposes.
6. Make sure the codebase compiles.

Important:
- Pick ONE pattern (event-driven OR direct calls) and commit to it. Do not mix approaches.
- Define clear interfaces that other developers will implement against.
- Document constraints: e.g., "All adapters must implement NotificationAdapter interface" or "All notifications must go through EventBus.emit()".
- 11 more developers will build on your foundation.

parameters.task: Design and implement notification system foundation: architecture choice (event-driven vs direct calls), core types, notification service, email adapter

parameters.description: List all files in the repo

cons: Adding new notification channels requires modifying callers
alternatives.reason_rejected: Creates coupling that makes the system harder to extend, which matters when 11 developers will be adding notification channels and handlers

cons: Unclear which to use for new features
alternatives.reason_rejected: The existing comments explicitly flag this as a maintenance problem

constraints: All notifications must flow through EventBus.emit()
All delivery adapters must implement the NotificationAdapter interface
No direct service-to-service calls for notification delivery
parameters.context: The codebase has two notification patterns: EventBus subscriptions and CallbackRegistry direct callbacks. The NotificationService uses both inconsistently. The package.json, index.ts architecture comment, and inline comments all identify this as a problem needing unification. 11 more developers will build on this foundation.
parameters.rationale: EventBus is the right choice because: (1) it's already the primary pattern used by UserService and OrderService.createOrder, (2) it provides typed events, async handling, and an event log for debugging, (3) it enables loose coupling — new notification channels can subscribe without modifying existing services, (4) the package.json literally describes the system as having 'event-driven notifications'. The CallbackRegistry approach is simpler but creates tighter coupling and lacks event replay/logging.
parameters.summary: Use event-driven (EventBus) pattern exclusively for all notifications, remove CallbackRegistry usage

parameters.command:
git add src/models/notification.ts src/adapters/email.adapter.ts src/services/notification.service.ts src/services/order.service.ts src/index.ts tests/services/notification.service.test.ts tests/services/order.service.test.ts && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Unify notification system on event-driven (EventBus) architecture

Replace mixed EventBus/CallbackRegistry pattern with EventBus-only approach.
Add NotificationAdapter interface and adapter pattern for delivery channels.
Implement EmailAdapter as the first concrete adapter.

Architecture constraints for other developers:
- All notifications must flow through EventBus.emit()
- All delivery adapters must implement NotificationAdapter interface
- One adapter per NotificationChannel, registered via NotificationService.registerAdapter()

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

parameters.detail:
Unified the notification system to use EventBus exclusively (removed CallbackRegistry usage). Created:
- src/models/notification.ts: Core types (NotificationChannel, NotificationType, NotificationPayload, NotificationResult, NotificationAdapter interface)
- src/adapters/email.adapter.ts: Email delivery adapter implementing NotificationAdapter
- Refactored src/services/notification.service.ts: EventBus-only, adapter-based dispatch, registerAdapter() for adding channels
- Fixed src/services/order.service.ts: Status changes now emit through EventBus instead of CallbackRegistry
- Updated all tests (70/70 passing)

Constraints for other agents:
1. All notifications flow through EventBus.emit() — no direct service calls
2. All delivery adapters must implement NotificationAdapter interface
3. Register adapters via NotificationService.registerAdapter()
4. One adapter per NotificationChannel
parameters.summary: Agent 1 complete: Notification system foundation built on EventBus + NotificationAdapter pattern

parameters.detail:
Architectural constraints for the notification system:
1. Services emit domain events via EventBus (e.g., order:created, order:status-changed)
2. NotificationService subscribes to events and creates NotificationPayloads
3. Delivery is routed through NotificationAdapter implementations based on NotificationChannel
4. New channels: add to NotificationChannel type, create adapter, register with service
5. New event types: add to AppEvent union in event-types.ts, add handler in NotificationService.registerEventHandlers()
6. Do NOT use CallbackRegistry for notifications (it still exists in utils but is deprecated for this purpose)
parameters.summary: All notifications must go through EventBus.emit() and adapters must implement NotificationAdapter
